word_list.word: there is no place like home (, wherever you wander|)
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ plˈeɪs lˈaɪk hˈoʊm wɛɹɹˈɛvɚ juː wˈɑːndɚɹ ɔːɹ

senses.definition: used to imply that one's own home is the most comfortable and enjoyable place to be, where one can feel safe, relaxed, and content

word_list.pronunciation: ˈiːst ɔːɹ ɔːɹ wˈɛst hˈoʊmz ðə ɔːɹ bˈɛst

senses.definition: used to emphasize the idea that home is the most comfortable, safe, and familiar place where one can relax and be oneself

word_list.word: a house is not a home

senses.definition: used to say that a physical structure or dwelling does not necessarily become a home until it is filled with love, warmth, personal memories and experiences, and a sense of belonging

word_list.word: home is home, be it ever so homely
word_list.pronunciation: hˈoʊm ɪz hˈoʊm biː ɪt ˈɛvɚ sˌoʊ hˈoʊmli

senses.definition: used to imply that even if a home is modest or plain, it still holds a special place in one's heart because it is a place of comfort and familiarity

word_list.word: home is where the heart is
word_list.pronunciation: hˈoʊm ɪz wˌɛɹ ðə hˈɑːɹt ɪz

senses.definition: used to imply that one's true home or place of belonging is where one's loved ones, family, and cherished memories are, rather than a physical location or a specific house

word_list.word: a man's home is his castle
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ mˈænz hˈoʊm ɪz hɪz kˈæsəl

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of having control over one's living space and the happenings therein, and the right to privacy and security within the home

word_list.word: every dog is a lion at home
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi dˈɑːɡ ɪz ɐ lˈaɪən æt hˈoʊm

senses.definition: used to suggest that people tend to feel more comfortable, confident, and powerful in their own familiar surroundings

word_list.word: every dog is valiant at his own door
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi dˈɑːɡ ɪz vˈælɪənt æt hɪz ˈoʊn dˈoːɹ

senses.definition: used to imply that people or animals tend to be more confident and assertive in their own familiar surroundings, and may show more courage when defending their own territory

examples.x: Even though he's usually very timid and shy, my cat turns into a fierce protector when he's near his food bowl - every dog is valiant at his own door, as they say.

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi ɐ kˈɑːk kɹˈoʊz ˌɑːn əpˌɑːn hɪz ˈoʊn dˈʌŋhɪl

senses.definition: used to suggest that people may feel more confident and assertive in their own familiar surroundings, where their behavior is unlikely to be challenged or tested

word_list.word: every bird likes its own nest
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi bˈɜːd lˈaɪks ɪts ˈoʊn nˈɛst

senses.definition: used to imply that people have a natural attachment to their own home or familiar surroundings and may find it difficult to adjust to new environments or ways of life
